title: phyllanthus maestrensis
intro: Phyllanthus maestrensis, also known as West Indian gooseberry or maestrensis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, native to Cuba. The leaves and fruits of Phyllanthus maestrensis have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Phyllanthus maestrensis include its potential to reduce fever, treat liver and kidney diseases, and improve immune function. Phyllanthus maestrensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and fruits in hot water, and it can also be used as a tincture or added to herbal supplements.
